text: The da Vinci operating system is used to perform a complete mesenteric dissection, collecting lymph nodes associated with an incidentally found ileal mass during a routine sigmoid resection.
The surgeon opens the sealed ends of the ileum and aligns them side-by-side to prepare for a side-to-side anastomosis, using a ligature instead of intestinal staplers for division.

The surgeon introduces a 60mm intestinal stapler into the open ends of the ileum to perform a side-to-side anastomosis. After firing the stapler, the anastomotic line is inspected for complete hemostasis. The open end of the anastomosis is closed with two additional firings of the stapler.
The surgeon grasps the edges of the mesentery and closes the small mesenteric defect using an endo loop to prevent future bowel herniation.
After the resection of the ileum, the robotic system is used to perform a sigmoid resection, with the surgical field showing no complications.
